Overall this is a straightforward lo mein recipe that was not hard to make and satisfying.  If making again I'd seek out ways to reduce the sodium, perhaps add in a bit of hoisin, and re-balance the noodles vs. vegetables.  I added extra veg and some yellow pepper as it was, but this is still quite noodle-heavy.  Would be good with bok choy, sliced mushroom, water chestnuts would add a nice crunch.  A squirt of sriracha and a squeeze of lime to brighten it up are also great options.

Used thin-sliced lean pork loin to make pork lo mein instead. Very good. At my local Asian market I had a hard time finding any noodles marked "lo mein," but found  and used Shandong noodles. Very good!
used 1/2 tsp of basalmic and 1/2 tsp of rice vinegar to sub for the black vinegar.  Also Mirin for the Shaoxing wine, and left out the sugar.
Loved it! Made this exactly according to the recipe. I wish people would like a recipe as read instead of all this advice and changes.

@Nancy I agree with you! I always make a recipe the first time as written. Then, I’ll make modifications as necessary to accomodate my fam or my frig. It’s not the recipe, folks, if you make all these substitutions! Advice on where to source ingredients, and techniques is appreciated, but not all the subs!

I made this a few weeks ago with substitutes for some of the sauce ingredients: sherry for the wine; regular soy sauce for the dark and light; rice vinegar for the black vinegar. We liked it so much I ordered up the correct ingredients which we are using this evening. I’ll see if we notice the difference. However you make it, this was a quick and yummy Lo mein.

Sake is a good substitute for Shaoxing wine. It can also be used to "velvet" the chicken. 2-3TBL of sake, soy sauce, corn starch. You can add a pinch of baking soda & sugar. Mix & marinate for an hour or two.

Good - and I even went out and bought dark soy sauce and black vinegar - two items I don’t usually have on hand - but not ‘wow’. I may come back to this recipe but I’m still in search of a more perfect flavoured sauce.
@Natalie B I bought black vinegar and dark soy as well. I was surprised by how quickly I used them! I don’t cook Chinese food often but when I do, I guess I was always subbing those ingredients
This was good. Next time I will make 1.5x the sauce.

If you cannot find Chinese black vinegar or Shaoxing wine at your grocery store (and if there isn’t an Asian market near you), both are easily sourced online (and pretty inexpensive). This made a ton, and the noodles sucked up all the sauce overnight in the fridge, so I made another batch of the sauce and added it to the leftovers. Gotta say… this beat the pants off any lo mein I’ve ever gotten for takeout. Will ne making this on the regular.
Finally bought the Shaoxing wine. Made a huge difference. Everyone loved the flavor and had seconds!

I used fresh egg noodles from H Mart, substituted 1 T balsamic and 1 T Worchester sauce for black vinegar, skipped oyster sauce (didn't have) used low sodium soy sauce, sherry for Shaoxing wine. For veggies I used what was in my fridge, zucchini, carrots, onions, celery and broccoli. Used boneless chicken breast. Everyone loved it. Will make again.

@MJ
In other words, you used the recipe as a jumping off place to make an entirely different dish! Interesting approach. Do you plan to try the actual recipe at some point ?

I live in a community with NO good Asian food, so I attempt to make it from time to time. I would use Asian peanut oil for this instead of canola, is that OK? I ask this sincerely, as there are so many good comments on this dish and I’d like to know.

Peanut oil has a very high smoke point (higher than Canola, in fact), so it is good for frying and high-temperature cooking in general. The main difference you'll find is the mild nutty flavour, but there is so much flavour going on in the sauce I doubt you'll even notice. (Possibly not a great swap for baking, mind.) Enjoy!

Can't give this recipe an honest and precise review because there were some necessary adjustments as it's the middle of winter in Northern California (actual north- Humboldt County), so didn't have snow peas or mung bean sprouts. Used lots of extra carrots, some lacinato kale, napa cabbage, rice vinegar for the black vinegar, sake for the Shaoxing wine, and some Trader Joe's Thai wheat noodles I picked up last time I was in the TJ's vicinity.
Can someone please comment with a the brand name of real lo mein noodles and where I can order online?? Even the Chinese restaurants in my town use spaghetti... :-(
@Craig S. https://a.co/d/2mrbsTu

For anyone still looking for noodles - I use Twin Marquis (which are made in New York and can be purchased at my local Asian Market in NJ, and at Wegmans). They are in the frozen section of both stores. So they are 'fresh' but frozen. You can search on Instacart for Twin Marquis to see if any stores near you carry them. This brand has about a dozen or more different noodle varieties at our Asian Market (udon, pan fried, lo mein, thick, thin) - I buy a variety and keep them in my freezer.
Added Teriyaki with plant based faux chicken breasts - came out well for a veg option... my son loved it.   I used malt vinegar as a sub - I think it also came out well.   Very tasty!
Where is the reply button? People are asking questions and there are responses with no way to reference the original question other than @. Please include a reply button.

I like everything about this recipe and will make it again, but with less noodles—8 ounces rather than 12. The flaoare great, but for me, the noodles overwhelmed the protein and veggies. Otherwise delicious.
I modified for what I had in stock and it still turned out great. Subbed celery for the cabbage, 1 T rice vinegar to replace both the black vinegar & wine, and used rice vermicelli.
This was delicious made as written and ample for leftovers the following day.

This was not a good recipe for several reasons. 1) flavor was dull 2) all ingredients cannot be found at grocery store so several substitutions are required 3) too much noodle to chicken ratio 4) too much time. I rarely say this about nyt cooking recipes but I won’t be making it again
We made this with local Texas fresh bay shrimps and it was pretty easy and very tasty as our guests brought them and chicken can be used another way tomorrow
just perfect as it is.  Absolutely delicious.

Great recipe. Definitely velvet the chicken with corn starch and the wine/soy sauce as another commenter suggested. 

I used a bag of cabbage/carrot slaw in lieu of cutting my own. Will use two bags next time. 

I also suggest finishing the chicane in a small portion of the prepared sauce and serving  on top  as it can be a difficult to get the chicken to stir through the noodles and veggies. 

Mostly writing this here so I remember foe next time!
This turned out really well. I subbed rice wine vinegar and dry sherry for the wine and vinegar in the recipe. I used green beans and carrots...I'd say any vegetables would work. Definitely a keeper.

Chopped baby bok Choy is a good addition or substitute for some or all of the cabbage. Add later for more crunch. Can add with bean sprouts. Haven’t tried this but something like arrow root or gelatin to thicken it might be good. Also added one whole Serrano with seeds for spice with bean sprouts.

Easy.  I added broccoli and mushrooms ... used more noodles and doubled the sauce.  I followed what others have suggested and used sherry wine vinegar and didn't use the Chinese black vinegar or the shoaxing wine.  enjoyed buy all and lots of leftovers.
Substitute ponzu sauce for black vinegar. Was great!!

One night with only a few missing ingredients: no black vinegar, dark soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, cabbage lo mein noodles nor oyster sauce. Did have thick spaghetti, cole slaw mix, fish sauce, sherry, balsam vinegar, doubled the soy sauce.  Put noodles in refrigerator per comments. Turned out pretty good for this non-Asian couple, though I'm guessing we'd enjoy the real thing more.
This is my go to recipe for a great weeknight Chinese take out make at home meal. My family loves it.

What can be substituted for Chinese black vinegar?
Balsamic Vinegar .
Recent heart attack in family necessitates revamping of our diet. Is there such thing as a palatable low/no sodium version of this dish?

You aren't going to get the same punch of flavor but using nothing but the reduced sodium soy is the way to lighten up the sodium in this recipe. Unfortunately removing the soy all together would change the flavor profile so the sodium can only be reduced. I personally would add a pinch or two of MSG too to up the umami while keeping the sodium low as MSG has about a third of the sodium content as salt and 5% less than soy sauce. You can purchase it on Amazon and a little goes a long way.

Made 2 substitutions. Used green cabbage instead of napa (the grocery store was out of stock) just precooked it for a few minutes before adding the other veg and I also couldn't find fresh lo mein noodles, so I subbed dried Cantonese egg noodles. Turned out great.
Made as written.  The sauce was too salty and at the same time blah.  There are much better stir fry noodle recipes put there.
Will try with firm tofu instead of chicken. Looks delish!
Made it for dinner last night. It came out awesome! Consider sauteing  the ginger and garlic first in the oil before adding the chicken and later on as well before adding the veggies.
It was better than good, but way too much work.

The sauce is my favorite for any stir fry. It was worth it to go to the Asian market to find all the ingredients because I make stir fry at least twice a month and they keep a long time. If you want it extra saucy, add a couple of tablespoons of water and about 1/4 teaspoon starch.
This looks exciting to make. Can’t wait!
we both liked it.  didn't have snow peas but that didn't prove to be a big deal
I took the suggestions of using Worchestershire and Dry Sherry as substitutes. I added diced sweet onion. I think it benefits from a little chili oil.

Thought I’d try tofu. It worked pretty well however I tried substituting spaghetti noodles. I  weighed out 12 ounces dry, thought better and used less but still too much. Also added reconstituted dry mushrooms.

It’s rather bland. I have a jar of crushed red pepper, which helped. It’s not bad with not so many noodles next time!.
Can you use just regular low sodium soy sauce instead of the light and dark soy sauce?

We subbed 1:1 Worchester for the Chinese Black Vinegar because even my local Asian grocery didn't have it. It threw the taste off a *bit* so then we added 3 tbs of water and a bit more sugar and that rescued it. Other than that, velveted the chicken for 30 minutes (velveting makes a HUGE difference) and subbed broccoli for snow peas. Parcooked both broccoli and then chicken for 1 minute each in boiling water before the stir fry.

Absolutely incredible. My friend and I ate all of it.

title: Fragrant Coconut Chicken and Sweet Potato  Recipe
author: Kay Chun

comments.commentBody: I just made this tonight for my wife and I. The recipe was followed exactly as written. The prep and cooking was painless. The taste of the dish, we are still talking about. This is an easy to follow recipe with high reward when served. Give it a try. And, we used ground turkey. My wife Jan’s only comment was next time I should increase the size of sweet potato cubes. We will make this again. I’m already thinking about how I might substitute fresh shrimp for the ground turkey.
@JW definitely going to make with shrimp. I’ll add at the end to make sure it doesn’t get tough!
@JW totally agree with increasing the size of the sweet potato cubes!

I made the dish per the recipe and I think it has potential. I think it needs more of a number of ingredients: double the spinach and scallions, increase the garlic and ginger. I think the lime juice needs to be increased but recommend tasting and adding more according to your preference.
Can I freeze leftover meal? Want to try this. It looks yummy but I'm a single and like to freeze meals for the future.
@Deb Lievens you could probably 1/2 it and have leftovers!
I would imagine you can. We make the Spicy Turkey Stir Fry which is similar to this, and it freezes just fine with our Food Saver.

@Deb Lievens I love this question. I recently discovered single freezer trays on Amazon and they are the best. I can finally cook a big batches of food and conveniently store them by single batches. (No this is not an endorsement)

So many questions already. 
Turkey? Sure. 
Cast iron is non stick if properly seasoned. 
Tofu? I'd use firm. 
Freeze? I've successfully frozen similar meals.
I'm going to try to make this with non-ground chicken. Yum. But there isn't anyone on earth that can make this in twenty minutes. :)
@Kurt GeisselI I was thinking about whole thighs as well. Please post how that goes

@Kurt Geissel

I grind the thighs in the food processor. I keep it a bit more coarsely ground than preground meat in a supermarket.
@Linda that’s not gross raw meat in food processor ? How do you clean it welll ?

Would it be possible to make this with tofu or a plant-based meat to make it vegan? Also, if you swap the canola oil for coconut or avocado oil and sub cauliflower rice for the jasmine rice - this is Whole30 compliant.

@Lynsey 
I made this with pressed and crumbled extra firm tofu. Other than the swap out for chicken, I made it exactly as written. It is very good but I think the coconut milk gravy probably relies on the schmaltzy flavor of the chicken fat, especially because the author calls for thigh meat. I ended up adding a tiny amount of white miso paste to deepen it a bit.

@Lynsey
Freeze, thaw, squeeze out the water, then grate the tofu on the large holes of a box grater. The end product amazingly resembles ground meat.

@Lynsey Have you ever cooked with tempeh? It’s a plant-based protein that would probably be a perfect substitute. It crumbles like ground meat and nicely takes on the flavors of the dish. And it’s widely available in most supermarkets.

Followed the recipe exactly except can't find dark meat ground chicken in my area. Added 50% more garlic and ginger, lots of lime and cilantro, and it still felt flat. Would add some red pepper flakes next time. Took me an hour as the sweet potato did not get "golden and tender" in eight minutes, more like 25 minutes. Next time I'd put the sweet potato in the air fryer for about 10-12 minutes, while prepping.
@Looseleaf I put the sweet potato cubes in a dish with some water and microwave for about five minutes to soften it up.
Would it be possible to make this using fish chunks, such as a sturdy fish like cod?
I don't see why not. I've had a lot of success in substituting proteins in any number of dishes I've found here. You'll just need to adjust the cooking time and make sure you don't overcook the fish.
I dont care for ground chicken and was thinking of making it with diced chicken.  Would any other adjustments be needed?
@Sandy Tucker  yes, this will work with diced chicken very well!
@Sandy Tucker  The recipe notes that ground pork can be used as well.
@Sandy Tucker or shredded chicken
Looks like a cast iron skillet in the photo. Does that count as nonstick?

Made this with freshly ground chicken breasts, made one and a half times the recipe so that I didn’t have to store the extra coconut milk. Consensus from the family is that it was weird, but at the same time really really good. A keeper.

Glad I read the comments here! I made some slight changes using what I have available in rural
Oregon.  Made it a solid 8.5/10 for my family. Starting with adding pre diced and frozen butternut squash and sweet potatoes to the pan with avocado oil AND butter. Swapped the ground stuff (impossible to find here) with cubed chicken thighs for the fats and seasoning with red pepper flakes, garlic, onion powder, smoked sweet Spanish paprika, and lots of fresh pepper. Can’t find spinach this time of year here so skipped that and ended up using the whole can of coconut milk. Added the potato mix after I’d stirred the chicken and milk together so they didn’t get too mushy. Used lime juice out of a bottle spritzed on top. Delish! Will add to the rotation for “non-white people” foods. Served with minute rice cause I’m a tired mom and salad.
@missmandi “non-white-people food” is an interesting way to put it. It suggests seeing not just food, but people and cultures in a very ethnocentric way. You may want to reconsider that.

@missmandi : another Oregonian here…I know of what you speak. if only the NYT would open a NYT Recipe ingredients store somewhere within the Pacific NW! But my expertise with substitutions is coming along nicely.
This looks like a great recipe, but I have never seen ground dark meat chicken sold (and do not own a meat grinder). Could chopped up chicken thighs be substituted, if cooked a bit longer?
Ground dark meat turkey is available some places and would work. I think chopping chicken thighs would be fine as well.
You can grind the chicken in a food processor, or even just finely mince it.

@Sheila Goldman
I pound 1.3 pounds of chicken thighs and had the butcher put it through the grinder they have there. Came out to just over a pound.
Could I use ground turkey?
No. Straight to jail.
@MORTON NEWMAN We did tonight using lean ground turkey and it was great. You might need a little more oil and salt because you’re not using dark chicken meat, but overall it was delicious!
@Trevor may I ask why? Lol.
This recipe is delicious! Only thing I had to change was I used almost the full can of coconut milk and let it reduce a little longer. It was 10/10
Would this work with ground turkey as well?
@Melissa I did this with ground turkey and found it delicious. I followed the recipe, but I added some fish sauce and more lime juice to lean into to Thai flavors of the dish. Yummy

I used boneless, skinless chicken thighs and blitzed them in the food processor until they were coarsely ground. That worked well for me. Overall, it just tasted a little bit flat for me. I added a few dashes of fish sauce and I felt that was a good addition. The hot sauce is definitely a must. And I used my much loved cast iron skillet with no issues, but it is well seasoned.
@J Osborn fish sauce is often the answer! And hot sauce…
Any good replacement for cilantro? Would basil work?
@Desha if you don’t like cilantro I bet Thai basil would work great!!!!
@Desha parsley works fine in place of cilantro, I always use fresh parsley in recipes that call for lots of cilantro since my partner has the soap gene

As others suggested...doubled (or so) the garlic and ginger, used the entire can of coconut milk, added a bit of fish sauce and a few sprinkles of Penzey's curry. Delish! This will get added to our rotation!
Easy to make. I roasted the sweet potato. Tasty..and next time I will add more acid.
Followed the recipe and it’s super bland. It’s missing spices. It’’s essentially a curry without the curry powder or paste.
Can I use tofu instead of ground chicken?
Would it be possible to make this with shredded chicken instead, as a reader above asked? My partner is a throat cancer survivor and can’t swallow ground meat.
I love this dish! Seriously. I could eat it every week. I upped the garlic and ginger, used the whole can of coconut milk and threw in half a diced jalapeno. Freaking delicious!

I made almost exactly as written EXCEPT used ground pork because I had it. I thought the flavors were a little too mild and subtle for me so I added a TB of Curry powder (Penzey’s Curry Now) and 1/2 teas of red pepper flakes.  It was really good & an easy weeknight dinner with jasmine rice
This was delicious!!!  I made it exactly as the recipe was written. Great weeknight dinner. Will definitely make again.

Delicious! I had ground turkey and instead of spinach I had some zucchini I diced up (added it a couple minutes after adding the turkey). 

I also added some cornstarch to thicken the coconut milk (wasn’t reducing as much as I thought). Other than that I followed the recipe. So yummy! 3 year old and 13 month old approved!

I agree with the commentator above who said that this is a good basic recipe.  I omitted rice and added chickpeas instead. I also added parboiled sugar snap peas. For spice, I added pepper flakes and chili oil, as well as nam pla, and grated the lime skins. Was delicious, spicy and had good depth of flavor. And, yes, I absolutely used the whole can of coconut milk.

You mention to first add sweet potatoes and then add everything else over it. Then when adding coconut milk, you also mention to add sweet potatoes. But they were already supposed to be in there as I could not see if you mentioned to take them out of the pan. Thanks
In Step 1, last sentence, it says to transfer the sweet potatoes to a plate.
"Transfer to a plate."
Transfer to a plate, last sentence in step 1. Your instincts were spot on.
This was good! I added a splash of fish sauce and used Sriracha.

Quick weeknight stir fry. Is it the most delicious, life changing dish? No. But it’s quick and the flavors can be easily punched up if you like more spice/seasonings. I made the following changes and the left overs are still good. If you like sriracha and/or hot chili oil they’re def a necessity here and complement the dish well. 

I used Ground pork. Although I wouldn’t use chicken, I’d use turkey if i wanted it lighter. And I would  season the ground turkey to taste with the recipes spices or some blend of sympathetic flavors.  For the pork I fried it with just some salt and pepper.  I did drain some of the pork fat. Left a thin coating in the pan for some flavor. 

I added some chopped water chestnuts. Adds a nice texture to the dish. Fried them up alongside the scallions, garlic & ginger. 

Served with quinoa rather than rice. Seasoned the quinoa with some Goya sazon for color and flavor. 

Used the whole 15ox can of coconut milk. Upped my scallion, garlic, and ginger to taste to account for that. 

I do understand the bland comments. But if you like adding your own flair, following the recipe is a good “base” to then flavor up to your preferences.

When I tasted this before serving I found it a bit bland, so I followed the suggestion of other commenters and added fish sauce. That—and the lime juice added at the end—helped immensely. 
Rather than using the whole can of coconut milk or freezing the rest, I used the remaining ~2/3 cup to make Tejal Rao’s coconut rice to serve this over. A winning combination!

It took ~forever~ to dice the sweet potato, but it was worth it (and at least it did cook up quickly). I was worried the recipe might be too gently flavored, but I liked it as-is, I didn't even add any hot sauce. I'm a cilantro-tastes-like-soap person, but I chanced it and didn't omit it, and it didn't stand out in the finished meal. This is a new favorite, it'll be going into the rotation (and maybe I'll get better at swiftly dicing).
Peel the potatoes. Slice into rounds. Slice each round into strips, rotate the round and slice strips into cubes. Goes pretty fast.
@Susan Thank you! I’d considered doing it that way but thought it might go slower. I’ll try it!
@Susan Can even skip the peeling part for certain sweet potatoes.

I liked it. I made it exactly as directed. I didn’t have any issues getting the sweet potatoes to cook in the 8 minute window. I didn’t bother finding out if the ground chicken I used was dark meat or not. I find all that stuff is pretty interchangeable and don’t stress about it. I liked the addition of veg, a great way to get some in. I probably would have used a bit more coconut milk and spinach. I also will amp up the garlic, ginger and lime juice next time. And I agree with others that hot sauce is a must. But all in, I would make again. It has protein and veg and some starch. Very doable for a weeknight meal.
I actually roasted the cubed sweet potato and threw it in after I cooked the ground meat. I only have a small pan so it made it a bit easier and I liked the flavored of the roasted sweet potato.

Somehow, this recipe defied all laws of nature by being delicious while also needing "something more." The hot sauce is definitely the coup de grace that makes this dish slay (ha!), but I think it needs additional depth during the cooking process. I salted beyond my comfort level and added ample pepper, but next time, I'll up the onions, ginge, garlic and lime and  add some crushed red pepper or diced fresh pepper. Moreover, I'm gonna be roasting sweet potatoes like this a lot from now on!

We made this with ground chicken, however I have never found dark meat ground chicken or turkey for those asking about turkey.  This has a great scent as eluded to in the title, but we found the flavor a bit bland even with hot sauce.  Would be better with dark meat as suggested or even just using chicken thighs. We won’t make again.

Although the prep makes this dish take at least twice the promised 20 min, it was worth it. I doubled recipe, used 1 lb of ground chicken (white) and 1 lb of pork. Two full heads of garlic, two inches of ginger. I followed others’ suggestions and after dicing the sweet potatoes I added salt, pepper and a little oil and roasted them in a 425 oven for about 30 minutes. That meant they were perfect to add in with the coconut milk. Also, that’s a lot of ginger and garlic- I started by peeling and finely chopping ginger, peeling and smashing garlic cloves, but then put both in a small prep bowl of food processor and that made them the perfect melt-away consistency (and saved time!). I used twice everything else- two potatoes, 1/2 cup scallions, and a whole can of coconut milk. We happily fed four people with rice and or mashed cauliflower and had leftovers for lunches. My 14 year old has asked that it go into regular rotation at our house!

With thanks for all previous tips, I doubled the garlic and ginger, added just a touch of fish sauce and a generous teaspoon of chilli crisp, and also amped up the coriander and lime. I think it would have been on the bland side if I had not taken those measures, as the flavor was still not overwhelming. I also found that the whole can of coconut milk was necessary. It was pretty good and I might make it again.
Added 6 cloves, 1.5 TBSP ginger, more salt and pepper, along with a seeded serrano pepper, kids and husband loved it. Easy recipe even for cooking in an RV while camping.

Really tasty. I upped the heat by adding in a couple Serranos in with the garlic and ginger, and some white pepper, coriander and cayenne as well. Obviously this takes closer to 40-45 minutes when you factor in all the time rice, chopping, etc. But it's tasty and filling and will make for a delicious round of leftovers too.

This was simple to make and tasty. We'll make it again. Did the naysayers even try it?
Used ground turkey and cut the sweet potato into 1/2 inch cubes. The mild sweetness of the potato and the coconut milk is nicely balanced with the lime juice. Didn't add salt and pepper the third time as called for. It didn't need it.
Whole family gave thumbs up, no way 20 min but certainly easy enough.

A couple things I missed as I read & cooked:

Remove sweet potato to plate after sauteeing
Only use 1 cup of coconut milk, not full can.

Hard to critique without following the recipe but i agree, the flavors fell more flat than I was hoping.  I will have to try again with the above steps.

this sounded like a great vegetarian dish. 
Until I got to the "Chicken" part.
Why-oh-Why can the Times food writers not include just a single sentence (for dishes that are close to V) "For a Meat-Free version, use X in place of Chicken"
I'll figure it out, but you could help.
Really enjoyed it; added more spinach, scallions, ginger and garlic and a heavy dose of pepper. Would add more spice and sweet potatoes next time.
Anything that needs ingredients to be peeled and diced, thinly sliced, minced and coarsely chopped will not “come together quickly.”

A keeper! Made this tonight, easy! It was pretty good when finished but I wanted more flavor so I stirred in a little curry paste and added a bit of mint and basil I had leftover in my fridge. 
I ate it without any rice, sort of a Thai inspired chili bowl - YUM. 
I want to make it again with crumbled tofu in place of the chicken, and a spoonful of chili crunch, I think it would be equally good. Versatile recipe that's easy, satisfying and healthy.
One of those recipes that is just a bunch of ingredients. Tastes like nothing!

Wow, made this last night and it was really good. Read reviews and punched it up with more garlic, ginger, and a couple of tablespoons of miso. Also used ground turkey (because I had some in the fridge). Swapped kale for spinach. Browned the turkey first, then the aromatics, then cubed sweet potatoes, and coconut milk (used the full can). Covered it and let it cook until sweet potatoes were tender, about 40 minutes. Added the kale, cilantro, and lime juice. Served it over white rice. Excellent.
It might taste good, but it doesn't look very appetizing (to me, at least).
I made it with pressed tofu. Excellent!

This is a great basic recipe. I amped up the flavor by adding a teaspoon more of both the ginger and garlic (I keep cubes in the freezer) and added Penzey's Bangkok Blend (a little more than a teaspoon). I finely chopped the sweet potato in the food processor as well as the arugula (no stringy pieces!) and cooked the sweet potato with the chicken. I used the entire can of coconut milk and added a few dashes of fish sauce, and some lime zest when I added the lime juice. Served with brown rice.

Made per recipe, but it was terminally bland. The recipe has promise, though, so next time I'll dice a pound of boneless, skinless thighs. I'll also double the aromatics and add a teaspoon of turmeric and a pinch of crushed red peppers or cayenne.
This is a delicious dish. For me, however, the prep time was more than 20 minutes.

2x made, 2x a hit! I think next time, instead of doing the lime and cilantro in the dish, I’m gonna make cilantro lime rice to serve it over. Maybe lime zest in the dish. This one gets big compliments from family and friends.

This recipe is very similar to another NYT recipe for Thai-inspired chicken meatballs, only it’s easier to put together and has sweet potato. The one tweak I made was to add a teaspoon of fish sauce like the meatball recipe- you won’t be disappointed! Will definitely make again.
I didn't have a sweet potato, but I did have butternut squash, which was a very good substitute.  Easy to make, and everyone liked it--will do again
Next time I’ll pump up the garlic and ginger. I liked it, but I wanted stronger flavors. It was easy and tasty though.

As others have said, the recipe is a great starting point, but I think would be quite bland if made as is.  Per other suggestions, I doubled the ginger, garlic, and scallions.  I added about a teaspoon of curry powder to the sweet potatoes and also about a teaspoon to the chicken.  I added a half teaspoon of fish sauce.   For a touch of heat, I added a good squirt of Ninja Squirrel coconut sriracha sauce.  With all of that, we loved it.  Next time, I would also add more lime juice, and maybe something that would give it a bit of crunch, as the overall texture is very soft.
Can easily substitute tofu for chicken. Just as delicious.
No to canola or safflower oil.  Use coconut oil or avocado oil.
Care to elaborate?
Add a splash of fish sauce
I did this as well and also added a splash of black vinegar.  I used coconut aminos as a flavor enhancer at the table.  It was very good.
Can this be made with tofu instead of with chicken?

There was another recent recipe for Sheet Pan Coconut Shrimp and Sweet Potatoes that used very similar ingredients but roasted in the oven instead of using a skillet.  Has anyone tried both?  Which one is better? I am curious how the sauteed method of this recipe might work swapping shrimp in place of the chicken.
I had some lemongrass on hand and added that which was delicious! This was a quick and easy recipe, I would definitely recommend.

Excellent recipe! Added a bit more sweet potato as others suggested and used the whole can of coconut milk because I didn't want to freeze the rest (the recipe calls for one cup).  Turned out great!  Winner of a recipe and easy to make.

So delicious, I didn't find it bland at all. I did add about a tablespoon of red pepper flakes when cooking the garlic etc. I used ground turkey and sub'd in frozen chopped kale for the spinach, and frozen cubed sweet potato because chopping is not it sometimes. This will be a repeat for me.
So simple and tasty. Would be good with ground pork, tofu, shrimp, mushroom. Will add to my quick weeknight dinners list!
This is such an easy and healthy meal for weeknights; it lacks some punch. Especially it being labeled "curry" but with zero curry paste or spice. Will try again but will add that green curry paste
Made it exactly as written but with ground turkey instead of chicken and it was absolutely delicious. It did probably take closer to an hour from beginning to end to make with all the prep and steps.
This wasn’t terrible but just a bit bland. I would not make it again. Definitely add more sweet potatoes, garlic, ginger and lime to improve the flavor.

Minus one star because it looks like a fresh pet commercial. But! It’s DELICIOUS, so 4 stars. I subbed kale for the spinach, both because I like it better and because I had some on hand. Added kale after the chicken was cooked through, softened for a couple min, then added the coconut milk. Great healthy dinner option, just wish it looked as yummy as it tastes.
I added 1/4 tsp of cayenne flakes and sauteed the spinach separately. I served the chicken on the spinach, which kept the spinach from getting stringy. Seemed to be good changes to the recipe.

This is wonderful. I accidentally bought butternut squash instead of sweet potato and it was still great. I've tried a lot of the five star recipes on this site recently and this is one of the best. Can't recommend it highly enough.
The recipe could be improved with some curry type ingredients, cumin, coriander, cardamom, tumeric, ground cloves, and chili crisp.

Definitely did not come together for me.  Sweet potato were so mushy and I even baked them in oven before to speed up process.  No flavor and tasted like baby food.  1st fail on NYT recipes and I have made ALOT of them.  But at least it was healthy.

Absolutely delicious in my opinion; kids didn't like at all so this won't be repeated in our family dinner rotation. I think this will be great as a meal prep option (make on Sunday, have for lunch during the week). Extremely adaptable. I used what I had: 2 sweet potatoes instead of 1, lemon instead of lime, kale instead of spinach. I used the full can of coconut milk and just increased time to reduce. I would probably add red pepper flakes next time but this was a huge win in my book.
Easy to make and popular with the whole family! I’m eating the leftovers right now cold  for breakfast.
Could I use chopped leftover dark turkey meat instead of ground chicken?

@Deb L, I freeze meals with all of these ingredients frequently. The only thing I see degrading a bit is the sweet potatoes may end up a little bit mushy in the leftovers. But I'm imaging this will still taste good.
Love!!! Eating it right now. Doesn’t need rice either.

Made half the recipe for 2 people and used ground pork over jasmine rice. 
I thought it was pretty tasty and very easy to put together.

This was great - didn’t have goulash do served with broccoli on the side. I felt it needed something? Maybe bit of red chile minced or dab of red chili paste , or minced jalapeño? . Or add some Aleppo pepper as desired.
Added extra ginger, lime juice and some chili pepper flakes because I used whole can of coconut cream for extra sauce.  A hit with entire family - glad to have a new dish for the rotation.

Made as directed except with the following changes: ground turkey breast, light coconut milk, and brown rice. Delicious! A new addition to our regular rotation.  Loved the 1/4 inch cubed sweet potato.  Never had them this way and they were delightful. Five stars!

I made this tonight, it was really a quick and easy weeknight meal. I found it a little bland as is so I made the choice to add a ton more lime juice and a little more coconut milk to make it feel more like Thai inspired. I’d recommend it!

Yum! Made it tonight, almost - for once - sticking to the recipe!

I used ground pork, and subbed in the dark parts of some romaine leaves, roughly chopped, for the spinach. I also used fish sauce instead of plain salt, probably about two tsp in all, and threw in part of an orange bell pepper that was kicking about in the fridge, chopping it about the same size as the sweet potato. 

My partner has far less heat tolerance than I, so I just stirred in a good glug of Chinese chili oil into my portion, which gave it some nice warmth. Like others, I might try upping the ginger/garlic/scallion mix a bit, and a bit more lime juice, but it was lovely just as made!

One note about the chicken: you could easily make this dish vegan by using chickpeas instead of meat. I eat meat, but I’m moderate about it. I eat a lot of all-plant meals and chickpeas are one of my favorite plant proteins. The mild flavor never outshines the flavor notes of whatever dish I use them in. I typically used canned chickpeas with no salt added and just rinse them and dump them in towards the end of the cooking cycle. So good!!

We loved this dish. I added 3 chopped anchovies (could use fish sauce instead) and a tbsp of tomato paste to the garlic/ginger/scallion sauté for a layer of umami. Followed the recipe as written other than that and it was excellent—bursting with flavor. Even my mother, who dislikes coconut, thought it was delicious.
This was excellent! Hubby asked me to make it again. We ended up using the whole can of coconut milk (why only 1 cup?) and 2 limes. Will make again! Even my toddler liked it.
This was delicious, I only made minor modifications, doubled the sweet potatoes and used the whole can of coconut milk and added extra spinach, scallions, and cilantro.
Delish as is!
I added part of a serrano pepper to the ginger/scallion/garlic mix. Gave it tons of flavor.
I used ground turkey and chopped onion instead of scallions. It was really tasty and different.

This is good as written but.... it's fairly bland. Second time around I followed the recipe but added  S&B curry powder, bird peppers, fish sauce, Golden Mountain and more lime juice. I also used ground pork. This recipe is a good skeleton but it just needed some oomph.

Made this for dinner tonight, had it on the table in less than hour.  I always read the comments for helpful advice and made the following adjustments: doubled the garlic, ginger, sweet potato and spinach, used a full can of coconut milk.  Diced and roasted the sweet potatoes in 425 degree oven for 30 minutes.  Served this over jasmine rice with generous dose of hot sauce.  It was delicious and perfect for a cold winter evening!

I made this and loved it with no adjustments to the recipe.  I think it would work well as a vegetarian recipe by eliminating the meat.  Serve over rice.  I like another posters idea of using shrimp!  I’ll try that next time.  It’s flavorful and satisfying!

Using readers suggestions I doubled the garlic, ginger, scallions, cilantro, lime, and used a full can of coconut milk. My tweaks were that after sautéing the potatoes for eight minutes I added two bags of frozen spinach and let them cook down so their liquid could help soften the potatoes. Then I removed the spinach and sweet potatoes and proceeded with the recipe as instructed. I added fish sauce and garlic salt to the ground turkey mixture. Delicious recipe and will be on rotation.
Good with chicken breast instead of ground meat, Red pepper flakes and lots of salt helps much as I try to avoid excess salt. Leftover coconut milk can go straight into the rice fat and all. Great.
I agree with those who enjoyed it.  It is a great base, but go with your gut! Add more of the ingredients you like and call it a day.
It was better the next day.....hubs' comment was, 'nothing to write home about'! I had to add a spoonful of chicken Better Than Bouillon to improve the flavor.
Considering all the interesting ingredients, I expected this have much more robust flavor. Don’t think I’ll make it again.

I loved this and my teenage daughter who resists sweet potatoes was very enthusiastic! Is it possible to sub in ground turkey? Also, I agree with some of the other comments and would add red pepper flakes next time for a bit more flavor. This is definitely going in the rotation.

This is an easy cook with a delicious and generous yield. The whole family liked it, even the 2yo and pickier 6yo. Didn't deviate from recipe other than to add crushed peanuts as a heavy garnish at the end and that, my friends, was a power move for this recipe.

I used most of the flavor-boosting modifications suggested in the comments, and it was very tasty. Microwaved the diced sweet potato before giving it a quick sauté. Would be good garnished with bean sprouts. Keep the sweet potato peels and make quick snap chips with them in oven or air fryer.

Delicious.  I used lean ground turkey because I did not want to make a grocery store run.  The flavor profile looked good but after reading comments, I doubled scallions, ginger, and garlic.  Again, delicious.

This was delicious, quick, and easy!  I used precut butternut squash because I was lazy. :)  Also didn't have any ginger on hand - I added a hint of cinnamon, but I don't think it needed it (nor did it need hot sauce)!  Normally I buy 99% fat free ground chicken, but I went less lean this time and believe the recipe was better for it.  Highly recommend - very flavorful and hearty - will make again!

Used a full can of coconut milk and chopped spinach into smaller pieces. I added the lime juice at the end, but felt it needed more once we were eating over the rice. Ended up cutting up a lime to squeeze over individual portions at the table and that was perfect.
this recipe should sit for about 15 minutes after cooking, it thickens and the flavors become more pronounced.

I love the flavors in this. The sweet potatoes took longer than 8 minutes to soften, and I intermittently added a few tablespoons of water to the pan to help speed up cooking and prevent burning. The second time I made it I amped things up a bit: added extra ginger, garlic, and scallions, plus the rind of one lime to the chicken. And I added fish sauce and chilli flakes with the coconut milk. These additions worked really well.
made as written but added sriracha at end for some kick and omitted cilantro because I have that gene.  It was easy and delicious but felt maybe fish sauce may have helped?

I'm new to NYT cooking so maybe my expectations are lower than others but I made the recipe as written (minus using dark meat) and didn't find it flat at all. I used 1/2tsp salt and 1/4tsp pepper for each of the first two seasoning steps and just a sprinkle for the third. Agree with others that the ginger, garlic, and coconut milk could be scaled up to taste, and I might next time, but it was good as written. The lime really brightens it up. Can stand alone without rice, in my opinion.

Made this last night- delish! Followed suggestions to double ginger and garlic. Threw in a tsp green curry paste while frying them. Used a whole can of coconut milk. One thing I hate is the watery residue when wilting spinach, so I doubled the spinach and wilted it with the chicken, as soon as the chicken was no longer pink, and before adding coconut milk. I let all the spinach “water” boil off first. Worked out great! Served with jasmine rice.
Kid approved! Everyone loved it. I had ground turkey on hand and used that instead. Delicious and flavorful. Will be added to our family favorites.

I've made this a couple times now.  We use spicy mix baby greens instead of baby spinach.  I toss the yams with oil, salt and ground Espelette pepper and roast the yams in the oven.  This adds a little kick to this dish that we thought it needed.  Great dish!

Typical recipe with overwhelming garlic and onion. Other ingredients dampened by their continuous use in all recipes here. Stop their use and the return will be an appreciation of the other tastes returning.

After reading other comments, I doubled the garlic and ginger and added a heaping tsp of curry powder and 1/4 tsp of chili powder. I still needed hot sauce.  I had leftovers and didn’t want to eat rice again, so warmed them and served with lettuce wraps and hot sauce.
My picky five year old ate three servings, and Mom and Dad (both foodies) enjoyed it too. If that’s not five stars, I don’t know what is.
I would substitute winter squash for the sweet potato if I needed to.
Even after adding extra garlic and ginger, the BF and I thought this was lacking both flavor and texture. It's ok -- healthy and hearty but also blah and mooshy.
Add a touch of fish sauce when you add the coconut milk.  Thank me later.

Made with ground pork. Would make again. Good and comforting but not exciting per se. Truman called it "thai-inspired hamburger helper". Both added soy sauce or hot sauce.

Next time:
- drain off some fat (if using pork)
- More / larger sweet potato chunks
- Could add more spinach (full 5 oz container)
- add fish sauce?
Made exactly as written, will be on repeat in our house .

Like a lot of other people said, if one uses the full 1lb/500g of minced meat, one basically needs to double all the other ingredients. I did and it worked out great. I also added frozen lime leaves, and a teaspoon of palm sugar. I also added carrots and (at the end) fried zucchini. Very good mid week dinner - will be cooking it again.
Add shiitake mushrooms, fish sauce, 3 limes, light brown sugar, two Birds Eye chili

I was so excited about this recipe given the high ratings, but it was bland and lacked depth. I considered adding more spices throughout - cumin? Wish I had. Hot sauce at the end is a lazy substitute for depth/spice. Next time, I’ll try that- and go with ground turkey instead of chicken.
I added some rooster sauce for spice and oven roasted the sweet potatoes. Served over brown rice. Super yummy!

This was a tasty dish, but as another reviewer stated: there is no way this is prepared in 20 minutes! What we really like about this dish is that it combines interesting ingredients and flavors. I did stray from the recipe to combine sausage and chopped chicken thighs as the meat - otherwise followed the recipe.

Very glad I read comments! Added fish sauce, red pepper flakes, and doubled the garlic and ginger. Super flavorful that way—can imagine the recipe as is might be a little lackluster. Still rated five stars! Will def make again. 

Also, I ended up using the whole coconut milk can. Kale and kale florets instead of spinach because I had them :)
I like it...but it needs a kick...other than hot sauce...maybe use sesame oil?
Can you sub frozen spinach?
Easy and very good. I used ground dark meat turkey, added extra spinach and ginger and garlic, and also added 2 t of Japanese gochujang for some spice and depth of flavor. Excellent!

This was quite good & easy. I concur with JW who mentioned increasing the size of the sweet potatoes. I tend to let the main course sit (or simmer) while getting the other bits done, so the sweet potatoes got a bit mushy. I always increase the garlic, and I think I could have done more (at least double), along with more ginger. Diced up a chicken breast as I didn't have anything ground & worked just fine. I increased scallions & spinach-should do so with cilantro. Hot sauce was a good add.

I typically skip garlic and onion as my partner has an allium intolerance. I followed advice of others to increase ginger and acid and also added some curry powder to my ground turkey. Fantastic pay off for a quick simple meal.
Simple and delicious. I wouldn't change a thing.

This wasn’t BAD. But it definitely wasn’t good. It turned out to be very bland, even after doubling the garlic and ginger. We do not stan sweet potato in this household, so used regular potato instead - I always expect the recipe to turn out a little bit worse when there are substitutions. But this was just.. not it.
Would it come out ok with light coconut milk?
Added some Thai basil, yellow curry, and some red pepper flakes.

I thought this was very good. Super simple but I did add a couple things. Diced red bell pepper, 2 tbls of tamari plus 1 teaspoon of fish sauce and 1 Thai chili. Delicious and easy. Also sub’d turkey for chicken.
I bet you could cut up sweet potato fires for an easier prep.

My boyfriend and I thought this dish was good but needed more flavor. I added some chili pepper flakes and we couldn’t taste much besides that, I would definitely double the amount of garlic/ginger next time. I could see making this again as an easy meal prep dish though, and call me nuts, but I think it’d be pretty good with a fried egg on top.
Adding this to my rotation!  This dish was so easy to make and so delicious!  I added more lime juice because I love the punch.  Can’t wait to make again!

I made this for dinner tonight and it was scrumptious.  I made a few small modifications like using a large sweet potato and the whole can of coconut milk.  The magic ingredient was a few sprigs of Thai basil, chopped, and added with the cilantro.  Plenty for a second dinner for 2 and maybe a lunch.
This was very bland for me. I added a bit of fish sauce and a bit of sugar to make it more tasty otherwise you need to add a lot of salt.
This dish is fantastic! I added fish sauce at the end, with the cilantro and lime. Will definitely be adding to my rotation!
I just made this as is and I’m underwhelmed. No spices? Looks like something I’d see on a weight watchers meal plan. 5 stars…did I miss something??
Excellent. Made as recipe calls for but added a spoonful of Fly by Jing Zhong Sauce for a tiny little zip.
Nice recipe. I doubled the ginger and added more garlic. A chili pepper would have been nice too. Simple to make.

This was a great easy recipe with an enjoyable flavor profile. I do think it could use some more spinach and sweet potato and the base of the dish lacked a little bit of flavor so it definitely needed the hot sauce but with hot sauce it brought it to the next level. I agree with the other comments saying it could use more of the aromatics. Regardless all the ingredients go together really well!!
Good as written, great with a tablespoon of fish sauce and an extra lime added to the broth.

This was just ok. It was really the odd combo of textures that threw me off. And it wasn't enough coconut milk to be like a curry, not dry enough to be like a hash/skillet, so it was just kind of gloopy. Literal slop bowl. 

Unfortunate since it seemed like an easy to digest, easy to cook meal for the week.
Definitely needs more lime juice and…something. It’s just a bit bland. But with tweaking could be great. More ginger? Lemongrass? Something.

Loved this dish - very easy cooking experience. It did feel a tad flavorless compared to other similar dishes I’ve made. I actually added a drizzle of soy sauce which added some needed depth to the flavor. Agree with some of the other comments that suggest upping the aromatics. Overall, hearty, healthy, and delicious.

Delicious! I used chicken thighs cut to bite-sized pieces so we could eat with chop sticks and give more bite to the dish. Thinned with some leftover chicken broth I had on hand.  Doubled the spinach per other comments —good move! Served over basmati rice. Loved it!! Fun to make too!

I made this exactly as written except I used two 13 ounce microwaveable butternut squash packages just to make it easier. I cooked them in the microwave and then put them in the frying pan with the hot oil. Otherwise, no changes and it was delicious over white rice.

I incorporated many of other readers' comments, and this was a major winner! It was a comfort food entree with very  pleasing flavor, and is a recipe that I will make over and over and would serve to anyone. As others, I doubled the garlic, ginger, and used the whole can of coconut milk. I also added a chopped jalapeño with the garlic; cubed two medium sweet potatoes; probably doubled the lime and inserted the zest as well; and tossed in a lot of spinach. I also added a shake of garam masala; 2 shakes would be fine. Ample scallion, cilantro toppings & a splash of lime juice gave a nice finish. Delish!

I made the dish per the recipe and I think it has potential. I think it needs more of a number of ingredients: double the spinach and scallions, increase the garlic and ginger. I think the lime juice needs to be increased but recommend tasting and adding more according to your preference.

The questions about pans - I just used a seasoned wok.  Added couple T of water about halfway through potato cook.  Used full can of coconut milk and kept heat on to wilt spinach.  Needs more lime plus fish sauce instead of more onion, etc.  Adding heat - Thai chilies or Seranos would be good.  Agree this is a good recipe template from which to work.  Will be working variations.

Really enjoyed this dish.  My family, with varying palates, made these amendments:

1. Add curry powder
2. Add chili crisp
3. Add ‘This Little Goat Went to India’
4. None

Everyone enjoyed!
4 hours to source and buy all the ingredients ;-)
That’s not possible. I found everything but the coconut milk at Aldi.

32g fat - count ‘em! 
Save time and just inject the fat into your arteries.

This was delicious. I do suggest grating the ginger and using the full tablespoon of it. Grating the garlic helps as well if you want stronger flavors. My coconut milk is always solid at the top. I used all of the solids and then enough liquid to make 1 cup. I had no problem with too much liquid. The squeeze of lime at the end definitely brightens the flavors. I gave the recipe to a friend and she and her husband loved it as well.
I made this with ground pork - it was great!

Totally tasteless until we sauteed mustard seeds until they popped then added onion with: cumin, coriander, turmeric, sweet paprika and cayenne. Could also use some peanuts and not very cooked red bell pepper. Now it's a keeper.
roasted the sweet potatos (2) , threw in a chopped serrano, some fish sauce-- doubled the ginger-- halved the spinach (it gets a little slimy...) it was fantastic!
@cv I also added fish sauce and a dash of soy sauce — great minds…

Delicious! Followed the comments/advice to cut sweet potatoes in bigger chunks (and left on the skins!) also added a splash of fish sauce and soy for added umami and depth. It occurred to me as I made it that it’s essentially curry without the chili paste, so next time may add some green curry paste when I put in the aromatics. Mixed a little cornstarch to the lime juice when adding and it thickened perfectly.
Per comments I microwaved potato first for 3 minutes. Doubled ginger, added 2 Thai peppers and stirred in mint with cilantro- it’s a keeper!

Stupid good. I doubled it and used 50/50 chicken/pork. Made the recipe almost exactly as written - the only addition I made was a splash of fish sauce at the end with the lime and cilantro. This will be a weeknight standby!

I modified this recipe a lot and thought it was fine. I used ground turkey and substituted green beans for spinach, lite coconut milk for full fat, and fish sauce for salt and stirred in Thai red curry paste instead of adding hot sauce. The ground turkey had too strong a flavor for me. I will go with ground chicken next time.
Used ground pork and only had japanese sweet potatoes (closer to regular potato in terms of texture, not as sweet). Diced into about finger-width sized cubes. Love! def repeater.

Loved! I used a little more garlic & ginger than recommended and added red pepper flakes at this point for a little kick. Added a diced red bell pepper as I was browning the meat as I had one to use up. Added soy sauce & rice vinegar as the sauce was reducing down. I think that extra tang from the vinegar was needed. Used the rest of the coconut milk from the can to cook the rice and the whole thing came out amazing! My husband was eating leftovers straight out of the pan as he couldn’t get enough.

I made it exactly to the recipe, (just seasoned the ground chicken a bit) and my hubby and I loved it!  I had leftover roasted sweet potato cubes that I added at the last minute which were yummy.  Next time I'd would throw in the whole can of coconut milk to have more sauce for the rice because there's no such thing as too much coconut. It would be delish with a firm white fish or diced chicken thighs.
The recipe is really bland. Bump up the garlic and ginger. I also added a little fish sauce and it helped a lot.
I added red curry paste and fried it off before adding in the full can of coconut milk!
Skipped the scallions due to allergy and used frozen spinach. Added Costco Sriracha Seasoning and Chicago Spice House Chile con Carne Seasoning. Delicious.
Very easy to make and very tasty! My family loved it!
I don't like cilantro. What could I replace it with? Or would it be ok to just leave it out without replacing?
@Sarah try a different flavored herb entirely, like basil. That would be lovely in this dish.
I used 5oz spinach and a full can of coconut milk because that is the way they came. A large sweet potato and a 16-17 inch carbon steel skillet. A hit with the family. Delish!

It was as quick as the recipe said, after all the chopping and prepping. Really versatile—used chorizo cause that was what was at hand, and some leftover chickpeas. It needed just a little something more, flavor-wise; I'm thinking maybe some heat, like smoked paprika.
Tasty and comforting but bland. The hot sauce made it delish, and definitely garnish with more scallion and cilantro.

This was very nice and delivered on the name! I was a lot more patient browning the sweet potatoes, no need to rush that step. I also opted to cook the meat first, before adding the scallions and garlic. I went for the pork option and it was a winner. Served over basmati rice and garnished with cilantro and radish slices. Really good!

This dish was satisfying and easy to make. But I feel like I over-relied on the hot sauce that I served it with to provide oomph and flavor. Next time, I'm going to add two hot chiles (like serrano, birds-eye, or jalapeño) when I sauté the scallions and some soy sauce (or perhaps even fish sauce?) at the end.
This sounds delicious! Someone sent us some ground pork, can that be used instead of the ground chicken? Also, is substituting cubed butternut squash for the sweet potatoe ok?

@Missy I wouldn’t sub in butternut squash for the sweet potato, it would really change the texture and taste. And I love butternut squash! But with the ground chicken/pork, the starchier sweet potato is better

Based on previous comments, I added more of everything: garlic, ginger, scallions, coconut milk, spinach, cilantro, except the amount of ground turkey, Unfortunately, this recipe was bland and uninteresting. Disappointing.
I had reduced fat coconut milk and coconut cream. Mixed them and worked fantastic! I liked it with a little more ginger. Made it in Dutch oven. We absolutely loved it! Easy and delicious!
This was delicious and very easy.  I used coconut oil to up the coconut flavor.
This recipe is awesome!! Absolutely love it.

I made this on a icy winter evening and we all really liked it. I used onions instead of scallions because that's what I had on hand, and also added chopped orange and yellow bell peppers. I wonder how  it would be to add actual coconut? Very easy and fast to make.
Sweet potato overwhelmed everything just a little bit too weird
Good! Easy. I added chopped, boiled green beans in place of spinach because that's what I had, and they worked just fine. Served topped with jarred jalapeno crisp, which went very well.

Is the "coconut milk" in this recipe the refrigerated carton variety sold next to cow's milk or the canned product in the Asian section?  Both say that they are coconut milk, but very different for cooking purposes.

This got a 6/10 from my family even though we all like the individual ingredients- just was not flavorful. Didn’t feel cohesive either. As I was cooking it I thought this seems like it needs more spices - should have read the comments! Hot sauce is essential but still not enough to make the dish good enough to warrant a spot in the rotation.
Quick and easy, esp. for a weeknight. Added peas too for more veggies.
Ready to prepare but not as tasty as I’d hoped. Kinda blah.  Will think about ways to boost the flavor.  “Fragrant” for sure in the delicious scent while cooking :)

Made this last night, it was outstanding! Made exactly as written with ground pork and the flavors and textures were great. The only thing I'll do differently next time is add a touch of heat -- red pepper flakes, or maybe some white pepper instead of black. I bet a wee bit of chili oil while cooking the protein would be great ... or at least a little chili crisp on top when served.
I realized I only have a can of coconut cream and not coconut milk. Can I use that instead?

To be truthful, it was a tad bland.  I used ground turkey but perhaps using pork would give it more flavor.  In hindsight, I think some mushrooms would give it more depth.  I tried putting a little Crispy Chili paste for my seconds but that’s was all I tasted, the .
Loved it! Added a splash of fish sauce towards the end.
I used ground pork and put in a little fish sauce toward the end. Some sliced jalapeños as garnish are nice, too.

After the sweet potatoes were almost cooked, I added sliced baby Bella mushrooms to the pan and they cooked with the finishing of the potatoes. It fit in well with the dish and added a texture and consistency that rounded off the dish perfectly. Otherwise, there was nothing very firm to pull everything together.
Quick and comforting, and absolutely DELISH! We practically licked our plates!

This was really great comfort food on a cold winter night. healthy, too. The only thing I did differently was add some curry powder (yes Penzeys like the others) and the full can of coconut milk. It needs salt to make the flavor pop so I dont skimp. Otherwise, i could see that it could be bland without it.I also added a pinch of cayenne. Perfection
Added shitake and chanterelle mushrooms that I needed to use, zest from the lime and a handful of cashews at the end. Perfection.
Made with some reservations, but soooo good. A little fish sauce took it over the top!

I made this tonight and it was delicious. Only things I changed:
Less spinach but added green cabbage 
Less lime (1 tbsp)
Splash of chili oil
Used whole can of coconut milk
Served with jasmine rice 
It’s a nice easy light healthy meal that tastes better than takeout!
It is delicious but I found the texture too mealy, so I added a few handfuls of crushed pistachios to each bowl. The hot sauce is essential.

Home chef, but would not stand a chance on master chef. Followed recipe mostly. Added a shake or 2 of crushed red. Used light milk instead of full fat. My wife accidentally added the whole cup of sliced scallions… but it worked in the end!Overall, was anxious about enough flavor. Trust in adding salt as you go and taste.  Served over jasmine rice, a few more shakes of salt… couple dabs of hot sauce here and there. And I’ll be… it’s really actually quite crave-able. Didn’t think I would when I was doing it, but I’ll probably make this again.  (Thinking maybe I’ll add lemon grass next time)
This had great flavor. I used ground pork instead of ground chicken but followed the recipe otherwise. For ground pork, I'll skip cooking it in oil next time. We'll definitely be making it again.

Eh, …. It was underwhelming and lacked character despite amping the ginger and garlic.

Just overall , bland.
Super easy prep though.

Thanks to all the comments, I doubled the scallions, garlic, ginger, and cilantro, added fish sauce to the chicken, and used the whole can of coconut milk. Additionally, I used around a tablespoon of Madras curry, divided between the potatoes and the chicken. I made this choice because of my sweet potato: it was a white-fleshed Japanese sweet potato, and I didn’t want the dish to look so monochrome. But then I figured I should just commit to making this a true curry. I highly recommend this! Also, my sweet potato cooked just fine on the stove top, 9 minutes, but it was a true 1/4-inch dice.

Very easy to make and tasty. I used Grape Seed Oil.
I would make it again.
Made this as directed and it was fantastic. The folks who found it wanting must have left out or cut back on the lime juice....it makes a BIG difference.

Made this for dinner tonight. My tweaks this time: roasted the diced sweet potatoes in the air fryer and they got nicely caramelized, doubled the amount of aromatics and added curry powder and cooked the chicken in the meantime. I think next time I'll try with ground pork and maybe add a minced serrano chile, perhaps use some green curry paste.

Nice flavor but not incredible. A good way to get some veggies into young kids as this is something my 7 and 9 year old grandsons would willingly eat. But we won’t bother to make it again for us. Recommend a full 5 oz. of spinach. I also added a bit more of the coconut milk, as it was a bit dry with the recommended 1 cup. That being said, I used ground white meat Turkey for the protein, which may account for the dryness. Served with jasmine rice.
Thankfully this was an easy meal to make. Since it came out bland I wasn’t too upset. I even tripled the garlic, doubled the ginger. Had a few spoonfuls and then threw it out.

Enjoyed this recipe. Used raw small-dice chicken breast, and parsley, because that’s what I had. Added a little more garlic and ginger and more scallions. Frozen chopped spinach, and voila! An easy, quick, healthy weeknight dinner.
I made it with beyond beef and it was pretty decent. I’m sure it’s better with chicken but wanted to try it since I had a pack that I needed to use. Definitely will try again with chicken

Agree with earlier reviewer to additional garlic, ginger, and lime juice is needed. If juice has  collected with the sweet potatoes after they are removed, don’t put that juice back into the skillet. We found that the dish didn’t have a distinctive coconut flavor and it May have gotten diluted. Taste as you cook, and add in extra of spice ingredients and lime juice, to your taste.
First time leaving a comment after cooking nytimes recipes for years - to say that this was the easiest and really flavorful and a total weeknight star.

I used ground turkey and shallots as the only substitutions. There’s a lot of peeling and chopping with shallots, garlic and ginger. Otherwise it’s a quick and easy dish. I roasted the sweet potato chunks on sheet pan in oven to insure their doneness. Really delicious over rice.

So easy and tasty. I like to think about recipes in terms of return on effort and this one gave fantastic results for the time I put into it. My kids asked for leftovers to take to school for lunch. I used ground pork because it was on sale and added a small splash of fish sauce.

Delicious but also easily modifiable. Don’t skimp on the aromatics and lime. Added diced jalapeño, a bit of fish sauce and some chickpeas I had in the fridge. Didn’t have spinach but I didn’t miss it.
I used ground pork. Next time I would use half inch dice for sweet potato. I also used  half the salt and pepper. I enjoyed the flavor.
Mildly Thai flavors. Easy to make. Don’t forget to add red pepper flakes.
Made with turkey instead of chicken. Doubled the sweet potato since I had one. Wish I upped the ginger. Won’t be a repeat meal for me but glad to have something different tonight!
Love this recipe. Flavorful, easy and leftovers!!
Double the ginger and garlic. We liked adding crushed red pepper for some spice.

Delicious!  I used ground turkey and bok choy instead of spinach and added extra lime juice and some makrut lime salt.  The lime is the key ingredient that brings the dish together.  I made a double recipe.  Great for easy leftovers.
Truly a bland dish, even with the addition of red pepper flakes. There are better dishes requiring 20 minutes of chopping. Don’t bother with this one.
BTW - the photo makes it looks more colorful than it is. Is that a yam or carrot? It’s not a sweet potato!
Cut a couple of chicken thighs into bite-size pieces instead of using ground meat, and subbed kale for spinach. It was very good but probably too much chopping to repeat.

Great recipe. Absolutely delicious. Made as written. To make more friendly for my IBS, I subbed garlic vegetable oil for the canola and left out the fresh garlic. Paired beautifully with a Dr. Konstantin Frank Gewurztraminer. Will absolutely be making this again!!

Fast & easy but very bland. If you choose to try this, I would recommend reading through the comments and adding some of the suggested spices like smoked paprika, pepper flakes or maybe curry.  I think there are plenty of other more tasty recipes on this site so I'm taking this out  of my saved file.

No leftovers - so that is a good sign it was a hit with the family. I used a can of coconut (instead of just a cup) and put it over rice.

Caveat - the recipe above is bland! I added fresh jalapenos and hot sauce - and it tasted much better.
I used diced chicken and it turned out great!

Delicious.  I did air fry the sweet potatoes first and used an entire can of coconut milk and let it simmer until the liquid thickened.  We only got 3 bowls out of this, so I plan to double the recipe next time.
For vegetarians I substituted the chicken for corn and it was still delicious.

I did not have ground chicken, but had the other ingredients and was desperate to use my chicken thighs so just adapted it. (not on purpose but trying to use all my food before shopping again!)  I shredded the chicken once it was cooked/softened. I added ginger and lime to taste. We loved it over brown rice.  I will make it again as an easy, delish dish.

Super easy weeknight dish.  I began the sweet potato in an air fryer while I prepped everything else, then I browned them in the skillet.  Used a full can of coconut milk. Definitely needs a bit of hot sauce, res pepper flakes, or Serranos.  Looking forward to leftovers for lunch.
This was okay. A bit bland as others have said. I added some fish sauce, which helped. Also, we topped it with Kimchi which added a good kick, along with homemade gochujang sauce.
Great recipe! New staple in the house. We prefer to use coconut cream instead of milk. Also seems to reduce evaporation time and flavor.
Really good and even better the next day.  Took comment advice to double garlic, cilantro, and ginger   Substituted ground pork for chicken.  Yum!

I made this yesterday evening and used ground turkey. I added extra garlic and ginger and the whole can of coconut milk per previous comments. I thought it was pretty good but my husband wasn’t crazy about it. If I ever make it again, I will used boneless skinless chicken thighs and try to think of something other than sweet potatoes to use. He really didn’t like the texture of the ground turkey and felt like the sweet potatoes made it too mushy.
Delicious weeknight meal, exactly the kind of simple, uplifting, unexpected recipe I hope NYT publishes often and forevermore! I made exactly as directed and wouldn’t change a thing.
Definitely increase the ginger, and garlic if you like me. I would also use more spinach. Otherwise, this was easy and tasty and came together, including Prep time in an hour total.
Tasty and quick meal to prepare. I did increase the garlic, ginger & coconut milk & added a splash of fish sauce. Next time I think I'll add shitake mushrooms. Quick meals are great!
very good
Would it be possible to make this with ground beef and regular milk and substitute the veggies and then freeze it?
@Just wondering you can do whatever you want!  Substituting the two main ingredients turns it into a completely different recipe, though.

I decided to use two sweet potatoes because I didn't think one was enough. One would have been enough. As a result, I overcooked the sweet potatoes because the pan was too full. When I added it to turkey, it made it a little too mushy. But the dish was still a good one!
Good. Added cumin to the sweet potatoes and extra seasoning. Used more garlic and green onion and easily an entire bag of spinach, as well as a healthy amount of red chilli flakes.
Our family really enjoyed this meal. My husband doesn’t like too much spice so this was great for him.
Deeply flavored? Nope. We found it bland, which is odd because mostly we find 5-star recipes deliver.

Really good.  Made exactly as written.  Like other users have noted, flavor was a bit flat or bland.  Wife’s comment was, “ This is a chic’s dish… or maybe a good post-colonoscopy dish”.  Lol.  Anyhow, some Gochujang fixed it right up.  Our rotation is a bit heavy at the moment, so a bit in-between if we’ll make it again.
Delicious and easy. I subbed dark ground turkey as that’s what I could get. No other changes except for a small amount of Red Boat fish sauce.

I made this tonight and the family enjoyed it very much.  I usually make a recipe as offered, but had more ground chicken than called for and so I added (almost doubled) all the ingredients.  I had shishito peppers on hand and browned them w the sweet potatoes.  After reading comments, I also increased spices w red pepper flakes, fish sauce, and curry powder.  Honestly, we could have increased the spices a bit more….and will do so next time we make this.  But, we all agreed it was a “keeper” with tweaking it to the family preferences for seasoning.

I didn't read the reviews before I made the dish and I'll say that was a mistake.  The fact that it says "hot sauce, for serving" should queue you in on something.  Its very bland.  My personal notes would include, more cilantro and scallions both in the dish and as garnish.  Sriracha should be in there as well.  I can't agree with 5 stars as is.
Super easy and so delicious. Will be coming back to this one, for sure!
This recipe was...fine. It was easy to assemble and came together quickly but something about it fell flat. I won't be making it again.
Made this with ground pork instead of the chicken.  Excellent!!
Strange recipe. Didn't taste bad, but everything about it was super weird.
Flavors were great. My wife and I both enjoyed this. Cooked exactly as instructed.
Absolutely delicious! Made as is (except used Turkey).
One of my favorite recipes I’ve ever cooked from NYT! Doubled the sweet potato, and serve with lots of lime and sriracha!

Like this recipe, but think it could use more flavor. I added some store bought tom kha curry paste, fish sauce, and extra garlic and ginger. Also used frozen spinach for ease and added beans to bulk it up. Yum!
Absolutely delicious, didn’t change anything. Tops!
My family doesn’t like coconut milk. Can I swap it for evaporated milk?
@Adrienne sure
@Sheila I use my food processor to grind cuts of meat and it works just fine. Cut the meat into chunks, 3-4 inch-ish, and pulse until you like the look of the product.
Agree, add more garlic, ginger and cilantro! Delish!
Personally, I needed to double the garlic and ginger and add some salt. Otherwise a perfectly fine one dish weeknight dinner.
so bland
I could make this once a week. We love it! We made it exactly as written.
Fast, easy, and delicious.  Followed the recipe but next time I’ll try it with ground turkey or ground pork, and I’ll double the ginger.

Not the prettiest but very tasty for sure! My 4-yo toddler had seconds. I had a bag of frozen leftover coconut milk that was slightly less than a cup but it worked out fine. Finished with a dash of fish sauce for the last seasoning

Some commentors shared that the sweet potato took longer to cook.  Suggest making sure pieces are 1/4 inch cubes - which is smaller than you might think.  Mine were perfectly tender, golden brown, and delicious!  My 11yo loved this recipe.
This has become one of my favorite meals. So yummy on a cold night.

I made this for my daycare lunch today. Most of the kids loved it (No hot sauce for them).

I used frozen spinach, and it worked well.

I'd double or triple the sweet potatoes next time.

The flavor boot from the hot sauce was wonderful!
We thought this was terrible. Bland and almost a nasty baby food flavor. The dish almost made me gag. I don’t understand how I got so many positive reviews.

Making this a pantry meal. I plan to use shallots for spring onions, lean pork shoulder chunks for mince (longer cook time of course), double aromatics with chilli for flavour and swap raw sweet potato for roasted sweet potato quarters with an Italian spin (mixed herbs, garlic granules, onion granules, Indian Kashmiri chilli powder for mild chilli smoky flavour, celery salt instead of salt and cracked black pepper). Maybe swap coconut for evaporated milk if the coconut is too strong. Will report back when I’ve made it.
Yummy, but mine needed more ginger, some red pepper flakes, and cayenne.
Might this work with bison burger?
This was really yummy! Really great combination of flavors I would not have come up with myself!

This is a keeper. Easy and really flavorful! As per some comments I added extra garlic and ginger, a little lime zest, and a heaping 1/4t crushed red pepper. I dislike cilantro, so I subbed parsley. This recipe is officially in my recipe rotation!!
I used ground turkey and used less salt but added a splash of fish sauce at the table....delicious, especially on  a cold evening!
Lovely recipe, did salt/pepper/lime to taste at the end was needed. We also added chopped peanuts as a garnish which added a good crunch.

We used ground pork but otherwise followed the recipe.   This gave us a very flavourful meal indeed, endorsed enthusiastically by all, including daughter-the-chef.   Two days later, the small amount of leftovers had a stronger flavour than when freshly made.
This will be a staple.
awesome and easy

I made this using already roasted sweet potato chunks - probably almost twice as much of that as the recipe calls for ( I had them on hand so I figured why  not). Definitely an extra bit of lime juice doesn't hurt, and we skip the cilantro because we're not fans. With the pre-roasted potatoes this literally takes 10 min to throw together, although I let it simmer for a while. It's even better the next day. My spouse requested that this be "in the rotation" from now on.

Easy and delicious. Used dino kale instead of spinach and just added it a bit earlier. Flavor was good until it was diluted by the rice; next time I'll up the garlic, ginger, and throw in a bit of lime zest and heat.

I was pleasantly surprised at how quickly this all came together, and I thought it was super tasty! Will definitely be making again soon. Start-to-finish took about 25-30 minutes, which is perfect for a weeknight, and there was minimal cleanup.
I substituted duck for chicken and arugula for spinach because that’s what I had. Super tasty!
